hero.label: FAQ
hero.title: Frequently asked questions about car inspection in Lithuania
hero.subtitle: How does a pre-purchase car inspection work, what does it include, and how much does it cost?

faq.q1: Is a pre-purchase car inspection really worth it?
faq.a1: You can skip it. Then you'll pay the mechanic instead, usually several times more. A car inspection isn't an expense. It's insurance against expensive surprises.
faq.q2: How long does the car inspection take?
faq.a2: About 1.5–2 hours on average. It depends on the car and its condition. We don't rush. Better to find a problem now than regret it later.
faq.q3: Do you inspect cars in any city in Lithuania?
faq.a3: Yes. We come to wherever the car is: at the seller's place, a car lot, or a service centre. No extra charge within Kaunas city and district. A travel fee applies outside the Kaunas area, agreed in advance.
faq.q4: What do I get after the car inspection?
faq.a4: A documented report with photos, identified defects, and a clear recommendation: buy, negotiate, or walk away.
faq.q5: What if the car turns out to be fine?
faq.a5: Great. Then you know you're paying for what you're actually getting. Peace of mind has its value too.
faq.q6: Can I be present during the inspection?
faq.a6: Absolutely. We even encourage it. You'll see the process firsthand and can ask questions on the spot.
faq.q7: Do you only inspect used cars?
faq.a7: Mostly, yes. But if you're buying a demo car or something “almost new” — it's still worth checking. Dealerships can be quiet about things too.
faq.q8: How much does a car inspection cost?
faq.a8: Computer diagnostics – €20, full pre-purchase inspection – €80. Additional travel fees may apply outside the Kaunas area.
faq.q9: Do you help negotiate the price?
faq.a9: Not directly. But a report with specific defects is the strongest negotiation tool you can have.
faq.q10: How do I book an inspection?
faq.a10: Call, write, or fill out the form on the contact page. We respond within a few hours.
faq.q11: What does a full car inspection include?
faq.a11: A comprehensive pre-purchase inspection covers the body (paint thickness measurement), engine, transmission, suspension, electrical system, OBD computer diagnostics, mileage verification and a test drive. You receive a documented report with photos.
faq.q12: Do you inspect cars imported from Germany or the USA?
faq.a12: Yes. We inspect cars from all countries: Germany, the USA, and elsewhere. For imports we pay special attention to accident history and mileage authenticity.
faq.q13: Which car brands do you inspect?
faq.a13: All brands: BMW, Volkswagen, Audi, Mercedes-Benz, Toyota, Volvo, Škoda, and any others. We have experience with both popular European and Japanese brands.
faq.q14: Do you inspect electric or hybrid vehicles?
faq.a14: Yes. We inspect electric and hybrid cars, including battery health assessment and EV-specific diagnostics.
faq.q15: What's the difference between car inspection and diagnostics?
faq.a15: Computer diagnostics (OBD scan) reads electronic control unit data and error codes. A full car inspection additionally includes body assessment, paint thickness measurement, suspension, mileage analysis, test drive, and a comprehensive report.
faq.a16: Yes. We use specialised tools and multiple data sources to verify actual mileage. Odometer tampering leaves traceable patterns that inspection reveals.
faq.q17: Can I have a car from Autoplius or Autogidas inspected?
faq.a17: Of course. Send us the listing link from Autoplius, Autogidas, or any other platform — we can provide a preliminary assessment and arrange an on-site inspection.

cta.title: Didn't find your answer?
cta.text: Drop us a message. We'll reply personally.
